q: The compound with molecular formula $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6$, which gives only one monobromo derivative and takes up four moles of hydrogen per mole for complete hydrogenation has ________ $=$ electrons.
question: The compound with molecular formula $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6$, which gives only one monobromo derivative and takes up four moles of hydrogen per mole for complete hydrogenation has ________ $=$ electrons.
solution:
Since 4 moles of $\mathrm{H}_2$ is being added for complete hydrogenation the degree of unsaturation $=4$

No. of $\pi$ electrons in $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6=4 \times 2=8$

subject: Chemistry
q: 2-Methyl propyl bromide reacts with C2H5O-and gives ‘A’ whereas on reaction with C2H5OH it gives ‘B’. The mechanism followed in these reactions and the products ‘A’ and ‘B’ respectively are:
question: 2-Methyl propyl bromide reacts with C2H5O-and gives ‘A’ whereas on reaction with C2H5OH it gives ‘B’. The mechanism followed in these reactions and the products ‘A’ and ‘B’ respectively are:
options: SN1, A=tert-butyl ethyl ether; SN1, B=2-butyl ethyl ether
SN2, A=2-butyl ethyl ether; SN2, B=iso-butyl ethyl ether
SN1, A=tert-butyl ethyl ether; SN2, B=iso-butyl ethyl ether
solution:
The reaction between methyl propyl bromide and C2H5O- proceeds via an SN2 (substitution nucleophilic bimolecular) mechanism. In this mechanism, the nucleophile attacks the carbon atom bearing the bromine (the electrophilic carbon) in a single step, leading to the displacement of the bromine atom.

The reaction between methyl propyl bromide and C2H5OH can proceed via either an SN1 (substitution nucleophilic unimolecular). Here C2H5OH is a weak nucleophile. During the reaction 1,2-hydrogen shift will occur and tert-butyl ethyl ether is formed.

q: Alkyl halide is converted into alkyl isocyanide by reaction with
question: Alkyl halide is converted into alkyl isocyanide by reaction with
solution:
AgCN is covalent in nature so it does not dissociate in to Ag+ and CN- and on nitrogen electron density is high,and nitrogen is free to donate electron pair forming isocyanide or alkyl isonitrile as the main product.

R-X+AgCN→RNC+AgX

KCN is ionic in nature, so it dissociates completely.

R-X+KCN→RCN+KX

Hence the answer is D.

Consider the following sequence of reactions:
11.25 mg of chlorobenzene will produce __________ $\times 10^{-1} \mathrm{mg}$ of product B . (Consider the reactions result in complete conversion.) [Given molar mass of $\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{H}, \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{N}$ and Cl as $12,1,16,14$ and $35.5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ respectively]
question:
Consider the following sequence of reactions:
11.25 mg of chlorobenzene will produce __________ $\times 10^{-1} \mathrm{mg}$ of product B . (Consider the reactions result in complete conversion.) [Given molar mass of $\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{H}, \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{N}$ and Cl as $12,1,16,14$ and $35.5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ respectively]
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{11.25 \times 10^{-3}}{112.5}=\frac{x \times 10^{-1} \times 10^{-3}}{93} \\ & x \times 10^{-1}=93 \times 0.1 \\ & x=93 \mathrm{mg}\end{aligned}$

options: Both <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> are correct statements but <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is not the correct explanation of <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math>

Both <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> are wrong statements and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is the correct explanation of <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math>

<math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is a correct statement but <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is a wrong statement.
solution: <math><msub><mrow><mtext>CH</mtext></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math> does not undergo unimolecular nucleophilic substitution <math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>)</mo></math> reaction due to the formation of highly unstable cation <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mover><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>⊕</mo></mrow></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> which can not be delocalized by <math><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mo>-</mo></math> electrons. It does not give <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> because <math><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> cloud is exposed, so, hindrance is there.

subject: Chemistry
q: $RMgI$ when treated with ice cold water liberated a gas which occupied $1.4\text{ dm}^3/\text{g}$ at STP. The gas produced is further reacted with iodine in presence of $HIO_3$ to give compound (X). Compound (X) in presence of Na and dry ether produced compound (Y). Molar mass of compound (Y) is _______ $\text{g mol}^{-1}$. (Nearest integer)
question: $RMgI$ when treated with ice cold water liberated a gas which occupied $1.4\text{ dm}^3/\text{g}$ at STP. The gas produced is further reacted with iodine in presence of $HIO_3$ to give compound (X). Compound (X) in presence of Na and dry ether produced compound (Y). Molar mass of compound (Y) is _______ $\text{g mol}^{-1}$. (Nearest integer)
solution:
The reaction of Grignard reagent with water yields an alkane:

$RMgI + H_2O \rightarrow RH + Mg(OH)I$

Given that $1 \text{ g}$ of the gas $RH$ occupies $1.4 \text{ dm}^3$ at STP, we can find its molar mass. Using the standard molar volume at STP ($22.4 \text{ dm}^3$):

$\text{Molar mass of } RH = \dfrac{22.4 \text{ dm}^3 \text{ mol}^{-1}}{1.4 \text{ dm}^3 \text{ g}^{-1}} = 16 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$

The alkane with a molar mass of $16 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$ is methane ($CH_4$).

Methane reacts with iodine in the presence of an oxidizing agent like $HIO_3$ (which prevents the backward reaction by consuming the formed $HI$) to give methyl iodide:

$CH_4 + I_2 \xrightarrow{HIO_3} CH_3I + HI$

Thus, compound (X) is methyl iodide ($CH_3I$).

Compound (X) undergoes the Wurtz reaction when treated with sodium and dry ether to form a higher alkane:

$2CH_3I + 2Na \xrightarrow{\text{dry ether}} CH_3-CH_3 + 2NaI$

Compound (Y) is ethane ($C_2H_6$).

The molar mass of ethane ($C_2H_6$) is:

$2 \times 12 + 6 \times 1 = 30 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$

Answer: $30$

Second reaction is E2 reaction in which rate depends on conc. of base.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match the LIST-I with LIST-II
List-I (Reaction)List-II (Mechanism)
A. Williamson SynthesisI. Electrophilic addition
B. Friedel Craft ReactionII. Free radical substitution
C. Bromination of vinyl benzeneIII. Nucleophilic substitution
D. Chlorination of toluene in lightIV. Electrophilic substitution

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match the LIST-I with LIST-II
List-I (Reaction)List-II (Mechanism)
A. Williamson SynthesisI. Electrophilic addition
B. Friedel Craft ReactionII. Free radical substitution
C. Bromination of vinyl benzeneIII. Nucleophilic substitution
D. Chlorination of toluene in lightIV. Electrophilic substitution

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Williamson synthesis involves the reaction of an alkyl halide with a sodium alkoxide to form an ether, which proceeds via a nucleophilic substitution ($S_N2$) mechanism. Thus, A matches with III.

Friedel-Crafts reaction involves the alkylation or acylation of an aromatic ring in the presence of a Lewis acid catalyst, which proceeds via an electrophilic substitution mechanism. Thus, B matches with IV.

Bromination of vinyl benzene (styrene) involves the addition of bromine across the carbon-carbon double bond, which proceeds via an electrophilic addition mechanism. Thus, C matches with I.

Chlorination of toluene in the presence of sunlight (or UV light) involves the substitution of hydrogen atoms in the methyl group by chlorine, which proceeds via a free radical substitution mechanism. Thus, D matches with II.

The correct matching is A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II.

Answer: A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II

Statement (I) :

Statement (II) :
